schemes.title: Mission Shakti
schemes.description: India's flagship umbrella scheme for the safety, security, and empowerment of women across their entire life cycle. Operates through two sub-schemes: Sambal (safety) and Samarthya (empowerment).
benefits: One Stop Centres (Sakhi) — integrated support for women in distress
Beti Bachao Beti Padhao — awareness & girl-child protection
schemes.eligibility: All women and girls across India. Priority to those facing violence, distress, or marginalisation.

schemes.title: Scheme for Adolescent Girls (SAG)
schemes.description: Focuses on multi-dimensional development of adolescent girls (11–18 years) including nutrition, health, hygiene, life skills, and mainstreaming school dropouts. Delivered through Anganwadi Centres under Mission Poshan 2.0.
schemes.eligibility: Girls aged 11–18 years, especially out-of-school girls in select districts.

schemes.title: Beti Bachao Beti Padhao (BBBP)
schemes.description: A national campaign under Mission Shakti to address gender discrimination, prevent female foeticide, ensure girl child education, and promote menstrual hygiene management awareness at the community level.
benefits: Sanitary napkin awareness drives in schools
schemes.eligibility: All families with girl children. Focus on districts with low Child Sex Ratio (CSR).

schemes.title: Pradhan Mantri Matru Vandana Yojana (PMMVY)
schemes.description: Provides ₹5,000 cash incentive in three instalments to pregnant and lactating women for the first live birth, to compensate for wage loss and improve health and nutrition outcomes.
benefits: Facilitates rest before and after delivery
schemes.eligibility: Pregnant and lactating women, 19+ years, for first live birth. Excludes those in regular employment of Central/State Govt.

schemes.title: Menstrual Hygiene Scheme (MHS)
schemes.description: Part of the National Health Mission, this scheme distributes affordable sanitary napkins (brand: 'Freedays') at ₹6 for a pack of 6 via ASHA workers to adolescent girls (10–19 years) in rural areas across India.
benefits: Subsidised sanitary napkins via ASHA workers
schemes.eligibility: Adolescent girls aged 10–19 years in rural areas. Free/subsidised through ASHA workers.

schemes.title: Suvidha Sanitary Napkins — PMBJP
schemes.description: Oxo-biodegradable sanitary napkins called 'Suvidha' available at just ₹1 per pad (pack of 4 for ₹4) through 10,000+ Pradhan Mantri Bhartiya Janaushadhi Kendras (PMBJK) across India.
benefits: ₹1 per sanitary napkin — most affordable in India
schemes.eligibility: Any woman or girl. No eligibility restriction — walk-in purchase at Janaushadhi Kendra.

schemes.title: Samagra Shiksha — School Sanitation
schemes.description: Funds state-specific projects in schools including installation of sanitary pad vending machines, incinerators for safe disposal, and gender-segregated toilets with running water to support menstrual health management in schools.
benefits: Sanitary pad vending machines in schools
schemes.eligibility: Government and government-aided schools. Benefit available to all girl students enrolled.

schemes.title: One Stop Centre (Sakhi)
schemes.description: Sakhi centres provide integrated, immediate support to women affected by violence — private, medical, legal, psychological and shelter support under one roof. Over 700+ Sakhi centres operational across India.
schemes.eligibility: Any woman affected by violence, abuse, or in distress. Services are free and confidential.
